steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "There, wildlife ______ is on the front lines of conservation efforts." The blank fills in "biologist" plus a name. The name Dr. Purnima Devi Barman is an essential appositive — it identifies which wildlife biologist, since there are many. Essential appositives take no commas.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "biologist, Dr. Purnima Devi Barman,": Two commas set the name off as nonessential — as if any wildlife biologist would do. But the name is identifying, so this punctuation is wrong. ✗
  • "biologist, Dr. Purnima Devi Barman": One comma before the name opens a nonessential phrase but never closes it, creating a comma splice error. ✗
  • "biologist: Dr. Purnima Devi Barman": A colon requires an independent clause before it. "There, wildlife biologist" is not an independent clause, so the colon is ungrammatical here. ✗
  • "biologist Dr. Purnima Devi Barman": No punctuation between "biologist" and the name — correct for an essential appositive. The sentence reads naturally and grammatically. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
